title: Segfault: NULL deref in `os__path_normpath_impl` (`posixmodule.c:6149`)
description: os__path_normpath_impl builds `result` with PyUnicode_FromOrdinal/PyUnicode_FromWideChar (either NULL under OOM) and, for a bytes input, re-encodes via Py_SETREF(result, PyUnicode_EncodeFSDefault(result)) without a NULL check; EncodeFSDefault(NULL) -> unicode_encode_utf8(NULL) -> PyUnicode_Check(NULL)->ob_type segfault. Reproduces on all builds.
notes: Clean NULL deref, reproduces on ALL four builds (not debug/ASan-only). result from PyUnicode_FromOrdinal('.')/PyUnicode_FromWideChar can be NULL under OOM and is passed unchecked to PyUnicode_EncodeFSDefault on the PyBytes_Check(path->object) branch. Fix: `if (result != NULL && PyBytes_Check(...))` (function already returns result==NULL to propagate the error). 3-line minimal repro: posix._path_normpath(b"...") under set_nomemory sweep (bytes arg required for the re-encode branch). Surfaced by scripts/ingest.py as a NEW site (unicode_encode_utf8) on the python-4..7 corpus -- distinct from OOM-0009 (unicodeobject.c replace:10783). First bug found by the dedupe-ingest pipeline.
